markdown.value:
Sinister Diesel is a leading manufacturer of high-performance aftermarket diesel components, known for premium quality, innovation, and strong customer satisfaction. All products are designed, manufactured, and distributed from our Roseville, CA facility.

We operate in a fast-paced, hands-on environment where efficiency, accountability, and continuous improvement drive results.

We are seeking a Manufacturing / Production Supervisor to lead daily operations on the production floor. This is a hands-on role focused on keeping production moving, solving problems in real time, and improving workflow and efficiency. The right candidate is comfortable being on the floor, managing people, and taking ownership of output.

- Supervise daily production operations, including workflow, staffing, and scheduling
- Ensure production targets are met while maintaining quality standards
- Identify bottlenecks and implement practical solutions to improve throughput
- Lead, train, and hold production team members accountable
- Coordinate with purchasing and inventory to maintain material flow
- Maintain organization, cleanliness, and safety across the shop
- Assist in developing and improving standard operating procedures
- Monitor production output and address issues as they arise

- Experience in a manufacturing or production environment (automotive, machining, fabrication preferred)
- Proven ability to lead teams in a hands-on setting
- Strong understanding of production flow and shop operations
- Ability to troubleshoot issues quickly and keep production moving
- Strong work ethic and accountability
- Organized and detail-oriented


Preferred:

- Experience with CNC machining, fabrication, or assembly operations
- Experience using ERP systems (NetSuite a plus)

**Schedule**: 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, No weekends, Overtime as needed

**Work Location**: In person at our Roseville, CA facility

Our business is growing and we need you. We are an established manufacturing company in the automotive industry and an online parts retailer looking for a detail-oriented and dependable individual to join our team as a CNC Machinist (Level 3).

Sinister Diesel is seeking a highly skilled CNC Machinist III to join our team. This role is for an experienced machinist who can independently set up, program, and run CNC equipment while maintaining tight tolerances and high production quality.

- Set up, program, and operate CNC mills and/or lathes (3–5 axis preferred)
- Interpret complex blueprints, GD&T, and CAD drawings
- Select tooling, speeds, and feeds for optimal performance and efficiency
- Perform first article inspections and in-process quality checks
- Hold tight tolerances and ensure consistent part quality
- Troubleshoot machining issues and optimize cycle times
- Maintain organized work area and follow safety protocols
- Collaborate to improve processes

- 5+ years of CNC machining experience (or equivalent skill level)
- Strong knowledge of G-code and/or CAM software (Fusion 360, Mastercam, or similar)
- Experience with aluminum machining preferred
- Ability to work independently
- Solid understanding of inspection tools (calipers, micrometers, indicators, etc.)
- Experience in a production environment with repeatable quality standards

**Schedule**: 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, No weekends, Overtime as needed

**Work Location**: In person at our Roseville, CA facility

Our business is growing and we need you. We are an established manufacturing company in the automotive industry and an online parts retailer looking for a detail-oriented and dependable individual to join our team as an Ecommerce Coordinator.

In this role, you will help maintain and improve our ecommerce website, support online merchandising and product content, and manage day-to-day catalog updates across the site and marketplace channels. This position blends website maintenance, product data management, merchandising support, and launch execution in a fast-paced environment.

The Ecommerce Coordinator plays an important role in supporting our online sales operations. This position is ideal for someone who is organized, accurate, comfortable working with product data and website content, and able to manage recurring daily tasks with consistency. The right candidate will be proactive, detail-focused, and comfortable collaborating across teams to keep the site accurate, usable, and conversion-ready.

- Build, update, and optimize product pages, including images, descriptions, specs, pricing, fitment data, and other website content.
- Ensure product pages and product data are accurate, complete, organized, and aligned with inventory, branding, and promotions
- Support website merchandising, category updates, featured products, seasonal promotions, and new product launches.
- Create and optimize landing pages for campaigns, promotions, and product launches.
- Maintain and organize a large product catalog, including variants, categories, filters, navigation, uploads, updates, and discontinuations.
- Execute on-site SEO improvements across product and category pages, including page structure, metadata, and keyword usage
- Work closely with internal teams and marketing to align site content with ads, email, promotions, and ecommerce priorities.
- Monitor ecommerce activity and site performance, and identify or resolve issues that impact conversion, usability, accuracy, layout, page speed, or process efficiency.
- Manage product data across website, Amazon, and eBay channels, ensuring consistency in branding, content, imagery, and accuracy.
- Maintain organized records and documentation related to website updates and ecommerce operations.
- Assist with order-related issues as needed and support marketplace listing activity when required.

- Proven experience managing a full ecommerce website with a large catalog (1,000+ SKUs) is required
- Hands-on experience with Miva is strongly preferred; experience with comparable ecommerce platforms at scale is also valued
- Experience updating and managing products on ecommerce platforms such as Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, BigCommerce, Miva, or similar systems.
- Strong working knowledge of HTML/CSS for page builds, edits, and troubleshooting
- Experience building and optimizing landing pages for conversion.
- Solid understanding of SEO fundamentals, including on-page structure, metadata, and keyword usage.
- Experience organizing product catalogs and managing the product lifecycle, including uploads, updates, discontinuations, variants, categories, filters, and navigation.
- Comfortable working with bulk data in Excel or Google Sheets, including imports, exports, and data cleanup.
- Strong attention to detail and accuracy
- Good written and verbal communication skills.
- Ability to stay organized and manage multiple priorities.
- Dependable, team-oriented, and able to follow established processes.
- Experience managing product listings on Amazon Seller Central and/or eBay.
- Direct experience in automotive / aftermarket parts ecommerce or a similar product-based industry.
- Familiarity with online retail operations, order flow, and ecommerce best practices.
- Experience with Miva specifically.
- Familiarity with conversion rate optimization (CRO) principles.
- Experience working with paid traffic landing pages and alignment with Meta / Google Ads.

**Schedule**: 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, No weekends, Overtime as needed

**Work Location**: In person at our Roseville, CA facility

Our business is growing and we need you! Sinister Diesel is an established manufacturing company in the automotive industry and a leading online retailer of aftermarket diesel performance parts. We're looking for a detail-oriented and driven individual to join our team as a Purchasing Assistant to help support our procurement and inventory operations.

We are seeking a reliable and organized Purchasing Assistant to support our purchasing department. In this role, you'll help ensure the accurate and timely ordering of parts and materials, maintain strong vendor relationships, and assist with inventory management. This is a key role that helps keep our production and fulfillment teams running efficiently.

- Assist in issuing purchase orders and tracking order status
- Communicate with vendors to confirm pricing, availability, and delivery timelines
- Maintain accurate inventory and purchasing records
- Coordinate with receiving and warehouse teams to resolve order issues
- Help monitor stock levels and suggest reorders when needed
- Support procurement team with data entry and administrative tasks
- Identify opportunities to streamline purchasing processes
- Work closely with internal departments to ensure purchasing needs are met
- Receiving purchase orders and verifying quantities and quality check the items

- High school diploma or equivalent; college coursework or business-related training is a plus
- 1+ years of experience in purchasing, procurement, or inventory management preferred
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic computer systems
- Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail
- Strong verbal and written communication skills
- Ability to multitask and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment
- Self-starter with a positive attitude and problem-solving mindset

**Schedule**: 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, No weekends, Overtime as needed

**Work Location**: In person at our Roseville, CA facility
